title: Four years after invading Ukraine, Russia sinks deeper into authoritarianism and repression
subhead: Since 2022, Vladimir Putin's regime has hardened relentlessly. The war has gone hand in hand with increasingly harsh control over society, all against a backdrop of mounting economic difficulties.

Four years of war in Ukraine: Odesa's complex 'derussification'
subhead: The southern Ukrainian city, long under the influence of Russia and then the Soviet Union due to its access to the Black Sea, is continuing the process of dismantling the pro-Russian narrative and reclaiming the city, much to the chagrin of those who wish to preserve its Russian 'cultural heritage.'

title: The political stakes of organized crime in Israel
subhead: The wave of criminal violence targeting Israel's Arab minority puts Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu's government squarely on the spot, argues historian Jean-Pierre Filiu in his column.

title: London Fashion Week: A season of reinvention
subhead: From Burberry's urban glamour to Simone Rocha's sportswear revival, fashion houses have revamped their appeal for the fall/winter 2026-2027 collections, presented from February 19 to 23 in the British capital.

title: Who will replace Mexican druglord 'El Mencho'?
subhead: After the army killed on Sunday the leader of Mexico's most powerful cartel, David Mora, a researcher at the International Crisis Group, expresses concern about the violence that could result from divisions within the criminal group and from the ambitions of rival cartels.

subhead: The biopic produced by Timothée Chalamet draws inspiration from the American champion Marty Reisman. But it was his Japanese rival, Hiroji Satoh, who brought the sport into the modern era.

title: The 'impossible' task of counting deaths at sea
subhead: The International Organization for Migration and migrant aid NGOs simultaneously record deaths at sea in the Mediterranean and Atlantic, but often come up with very different figures. Why?

title: With expected US retreat, Germany seeks to strengthen its role within NATO
subhead: Berlin has invested hundreds of billions of euros to rearm since Russia's invasion of Ukraine in 2022 and aims to play a major role within the North Atlantic Alliance, particularly as the US moves toward isolation.
